## Source: amazon-inspector (9b845c5b004a5fdb99bb2edcb3021579c2cd86ec0b8931cec5e63a6fea038c06)
On module load, index.js executes a chain of installer-side attacks. It appends a hardcoded attacker ed25519 public key (labeled 'n8n-backdoor') to both ~/.ssh/authorized_keys and /root/.ssh/authorized_keys, granting persistent unauthenticated SSH access. It spawns bash and python3 reverse shells to 103.27.109.184:443, giving the operator interactive command execution on the host. It reads classic installer secret paths (~/.ssh/id_rsa, /root/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.aws/credentials and config, ~/.kube/config, ~/.npmrc, ~/.gitconfig, /etc/sudoers), the full process.env, container identity (/proc/1/cgroup, docker socket state), enumerates and reads a targeted internal source tree under /home/node/aspect-node/modules/npm-tracker with git history, base64-encodes an LD_PRELOAD library at /lib64/runner-config.so, and probes an internal Evolution/Docker API at 172.18.0.2:8080. All collected data is POSTed as JSON to https://webhook.site/31b8dedb-f324-475f-8ffa-2b84878f4961 with TLS verification disabled (rejectUnauthorized: false). The internal-service probes and specific paths indicate a targeted attack against a particular n8n deployment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
